subject: Biology
chapter: Strategies for Enhancement in Food Production

questions.q: What is the main objective of crop improvement techniques?
questions.explanation: The main objective of crop improvement techniques is to increase crop yield, improve crop quality, and reduce crop diseases. Therefore, all of the above options are correct.
questions.topic: Introduction to Strategies for Enhancement in Food Production

questions.q: Which of the following is a method of crop improvement?
questions.explanation: All of the above options are methods of crop improvement. Hybridization involves crossing two different crop varieties to produce a hybrid with desirable traits. Mutation breeding involves inducing mutations in crops to produce new traits. Biotechnology involves the use of genetic engineering to introduce desirable traits into crops.

questions.q: What is the term for the introduction of desirable traits into crops through genetic engineering?
questions.explanation: Biotechnology is the term used to describe the introduction of desirable traits into crops through genetic engineering. This involves the use of recombinant DNA technology to introduce genes from one organism into another.

questions.q: Which of the following is an advantage of hybrid seeds?
opts: (A) They are cheaper than traditional seeds
(B) They have higher yields than traditional seeds
(C) They are more resistant to diseases than traditional seeds
(D) They require more water than traditional seeds
questions.explanation: Hybrid seeds have higher yields than traditional seeds due to the phenomenon of hybrid vigor. This is because the combination of genetic material from two different parent lines results in offspring that are more robust and have higher yields.

questions.q: What is the purpose of tissue culture in plant biotechnology?
opts: (A) To produce large quantities of plants
(B) To produce plants with desirable traits
(C) To study plant growth and development
questions.explanation: Tissue culture is a technique used in plant biotechnology to produce large quantities of plants, produce plants with desirable traits, and study plant growth and development. It involves the growth of plant cells or tissues in a controlled laboratory setting.

questions.q: Which of the following is a type of mutation breeding?
questions.explanation: All of the above options are types of mutation breeding. Physical mutagenesis involves the use of physical agents such as radiation to induce mutations. Chemical mutagenesis involves the use of chemical agents to induce mutations. Biological mutagenesis involves the use of biological agents such as viruses to induce mutations.

questions.q: What is the term for the process of breeding crops to be resistant to pests and diseases?
questions.explanation: Integrated pest management is the term used to describe the process of breeding crops to be resistant to pests and diseases. This involves the use of a combination of techniques such as crop rotation, biological control, and chemical control to manage pests and diseases.

questions.q: Which of the following is an advantage of organic farming?
opts: (A) It is more expensive than conventional farming
(B) It is less environmentally friendly than conventional farming
(C) It produces higher yields than conventional farming
(D) It is more sustainable than conventional farming
questions.explanation: Organic farming is more sustainable than conventional farming because it avoids the use of synthetic fertilizers and pesticides, which can harm the environment. Instead, organic farming relies on natural methods of crop rotation, composting, and biological control to maintain soil fertility and control pests and diseases.

questions.q: What is the term for the process of breeding animals to improve their productivity and quality?
questions.explanation: Animal breeding is the term used to describe the process of breeding animals to improve their productivity and quality. This involves the selection of animals with desirable traits and breeding them to produce offspring with those traits.

questions.q: Which of the following is a method of animal breeding?
questions.explanation: All of the above options are methods of animal breeding. Inbreeding involves the breeding of animals that are closely related to each other. Outbreeding involves the breeding of animals that are not closely related to each other. Crossbreeding involves the breeding of animals from different breeds or species.

questions.q: What is the term for the introduction of desirable traits into animals through genetic engineering?
questions.explanation: Transgenics is the term used to describe the introduction of desirable traits into animals through genetic engineering. This involves the use of recombinant DNA technology to introduce genes from one organism into another.

questions.q: Which of the following is an application of biotechnology in animal improvement?
questions.explanation: All of the above options are applications of biotechnology in animal improvement. Biotechnology has been used to produce vaccines, transgenic animals, and monoclonal antibodies, which have improved animal health and productivity.
questions.topic: Applications of Biotechnology in Animal Improvement

questions.q: What is the term for the process of breeding fish to improve their productivity and quality?
questions.explanation: Fish breeding is the term used to describe the process of breeding fish to improve their productivity and quality. This involves the selection of fish with desirable traits and breeding them to produce offspring with those traits.

questions.q: Which of the following is a method of fish breeding?
questions.explanation: All of the above options are methods of fish breeding. Selective breeding involves the selection of fish with desirable traits and breeding them to produce offspring with those traits. Hybridization involves the breeding of fish from different species or breeds. Crossbreeding involves the breeding of fish from different breeds or species.

questions.q: What is the term for the introduction of desirable traits into fish through genetic engineering?
questions.explanation: Transgenics is the term used to describe the introduction of desirable traits into fish through genetic engineering. This involves the use of recombinant DNA technology to introduce genes from one organism into another.

questions.q: Which of the following is an application of biotechnology in fish improvement?
questions.explanation: All of the above options are applications of biotechnology in fish improvement. Biotechnology has been used to produce vaccines, transgenic fish, and monoclonal antibodies, which have improved fish health and productivity.
questions.topic: Applications of Biotechnology in Fish Improvement

questions.q: What is the term for the process of breeding honeybees to improve their productivity and quality?
questions.explanation: Bee breeding is the term used to describe the process of breeding honeybees to improve their productivity and quality. This involves the selection of bees with desirable traits and breeding them to produce offspring with those traits.

questions.q: Which of the following is a method of bee breeding?
questions.explanation: All of the above options are methods of bee breeding. Selective breeding involves the selection of bees with desirable traits and breeding them to produce offspring with those traits. Hybridization involves the breeding of bees from different species or breeds. Crossbreeding involves the breeding of bees from different breeds or species.

questions.q: What is the main challenge in implementing strategies for enhancement in food production?
questions.explanation: The main challenge in implementing strategies for enhancement in food production is the lack of funds, technology, and skilled personnel. These factors can limit the ability to adopt new technologies and practices, and can hinder the effectiveness of strategies for enhancement in food production.
questions.topic: Challenges in Implementing Strategies for Enhancement in Food Production
